rationale: The signals show strong, consistent demand across Google Trends (rising queries for "crested gecko colors," "crested gecko care sheet," "black crested gecko," "blue crested gecko" with +40-300% growth), Google autocomplete volume, and YouTube engagement (67 videos, many focused on morphs and breeding). However, most queries cluster around general care, morph identification, and established morphs (Cappuccino, Lilly White, Axanthic). The Bluesky feed shows heavy MorphMarket listings and enthusiast engagement but no substantive forum debate or emerging controversies. We've already published on cappuccino, super-cappuccino, lilly-white, axanthic, and identification. The opportunity lies in emerging or under-covered color trends (black/blue morphs) and breeding misconceptions that YouTube hints at ("WARNING NEVER BREED THESE," "Huge Mistake or Hidden Opportunity?"). A deep genetics topic on color recessive/polygenic pairings, or a practical breeder guide on color morphing pitfalls, could fill a gap without duplicating existing content. I'm selecting one strong candidate that threads together the rising color interest, genetics grounding, and a correctable misconception.
